prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='FT_catboost', seasonality_champion='ONLY_FT_svr', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.913) and high seasonality variance (1.07), with FT_catboost leading trend tracking and ONLY_FT_svr capturing seasonality best. Early horizon specialists (THETA, ETS, ARIMA) differ from late horizon specialists (NaiveSeasonal, ARIMA, CWT_svr), indicating strong per-horizon specialization patterns. The data-driven recommendation is DBA combination to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating individual weaknesses across varying trend directions and seasonal patterns.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "FT_catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "ONLY_FT_svr",
  "overall_champion": "ONLY_CWT_rf",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "THETA", "late": "NaiveSeasonal"},
  "tier1_models": ["ONLY_CWT_rf", "THETA", "ETS", "ARIMA", "CWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "CWT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_DWT_catboost", "DWT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "FT_catboost", "rf", "FT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.913,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "down", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.913) and high seasonality variance (1.07), with FT_catboost leading trend tracking and ONLY_FT_svr capturing seasonality best. Early horizon specialists (THETA, ETS, ARIMA) differ from late horizon specialists (NaiveSeasonal, ARIMA, CWT_svr), indicating strong per-horizon specialization patterns. The data-driven recommendation is DBA combination to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating individual weaknesses across varying trend directions and seasonal patterns."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=9 indicates strong per-horizon specialization patterns requiring best_per_horizon selection. RMSE spread_ratio=0.913 (very high) favors top-k over full mean with shrinkage≥0.3 for n_windows=3. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality_variance=1.07 and heterogeneous horizons. inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35 provides robust weighted averaging with conservative shrinkage. score_preset=rmse_focus aligns with high model dispersion."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No leakage detected - dba_combination is baseline (no fitting), selection strategies use past windows only. Universe leaderboards show no non-baseline candidate beats all current candidates (trimmed_mean_r0.10 RMSE=6522, robust_median SMAPE=0.06716 are worse than current top scores). No redundancy pruning needed (<4 of same type). Set is stable with n_windows=3.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.913) and high seasonality variance (1.07), with FT_catboost leading trend tracking and ONLY_FT_svr capturing seasonality best. Early horizon specialists (THETA, ETS, ARIMA) differ from late horizon specialists (NaiveSeasonal, ARIMA, CWT_svr), indicating strong per-horizon specialization patterns. The data-driven recommendation is DBA combination to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating individual weaknesses across varying trend directions and seasonal patterns.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=9 indicates strong per-horizon specialization patterns requiring best_per_horizon selection. RMSE spread_ratio=0.913 (very high) favors top-k over full mean with shrinkage≥0.3 for n_windows=3. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality_variance=1.07 and heterogeneous horizons. inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35 provides robust weighted averaging with conservative shrinkage. score_preset=rmse_focus aligns with high model dispersion.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No leakage detected - dba_combination is baseline (no fitting), selection strategies use past windows only. Universe leaderboards show no non-baseline candidate beats all current candidates (trimmed_mean_r0.10 RMSE=6522, robust_median SMAPE=0.06716 are worse than current top scores). No redundancy pruning needed (<4 of same type). Set is stable with n_windows=3.
